description: Extracts key fields from Commercial Real Estate appraisal reports including property details, valuation approaches, and appraiser information.

Date_Of_Valuation.description: Date of the valuation or appraisal. Usually found on the cover page or in the preamble.

Subject_Property.description: Name or description of the subject property being appraised.

Property_Address.description: Full street address of the property being appraised, including street number and street name, city, state, and ZIP code. Look in the 'IDENTIFICATION OF THE PROPERTY' section or similar property description sections for phrases like 'known as', 'located at', or 'property address'. Must include the street-level address (e.g., '5950 Netherland Avenue, Bronx, NY'), not just the city or county.

Client_Name.description: Name of the client who commissioned the appraisal report.

Appraiser_Name_And_Certification.description: Appraiser's name and certification/license details as stated in the report.

Property_Type_And_Location.description: Type of property (e.g., office, retail, multifamily, industrial) and its general location description.

Final_Value_Conclusion.description: Find the final opinion of market value from the transmittal letter or cover letter at the beginning of the report. The transmittal letter typically states 'the Market Value of the subject property in its As Is condition is:' followed by the dollar amount in words and parentheses (e.g., 'Twenty Three Million Dollars ($23,000,000)'). Extract ONLY this value. Do NOT use 'VALUE VIA THE MARKET APPROACH', 'VALUE VIA THE INCOME APPROACH', or any individual valuation approach figure that appears later in the report - those are intermediate calculations. Return the dollar amount from the transmittal letter.

Effective_Date_Of_Appraisal.description: The effective date of the appraisal. This is a DERIVED value: find the date of the appraisal inspection or valuation (e.g., 'Date of Value & Property Inspection', 'as of March 20, 2001', or the appraisal date in the transmittal letter), then calculate the LAST DAY of that month. For example: if the inspection date is March 20, 2001, the effective date is 03/31/2001. If the date is 5/16/2016, the effective date is 05/31/2016. Always return the last calendar day of the month in which the appraisal was conducted, in MM/DD/YYYY format.

Legal_Description.description: Legal description of the property including lot, block, subdivision, or metes and bounds reference.

Parcel_Number.description: Tax parcel number or assessor's parcel number (APN) for the subject property.

Ownership_History.description: Recent ownership and sales history of the property, typically covering the last 3-5 years.

Appraisal_Purpose.description: Stated purpose of the appraisal (e.g., mortgage lending, estate planning, litigation support).

Current_Zoning_Classification.description: Current zoning classification of the property (e.g., C-2 Commercial, R-1 Residential, M-1 Industrial).

Lot_Size.description: Lot size, shape, and topography of the subject property. Include acreage or square footage as stated.

Flood_Zone.description: Flood zone designation for the subject property. Look in the 'Summary of Salient Facts and Conclusions', 'Site Description', 'Site Analysis', or 'Neighborhood Analysis' sections. Search for labels like 'Flood Map', 'Flood Zone', or 'FEMA' followed by the zone classification (e.g., 'Zone C', 'Zone X', 'Zone A', 'Zone AE'). Also check for references to FIRM panels or community panel numbers. Return the flood zone designation (e.g., 'Zone C'). Do NOT look in Demographics or Business Diversity sections.

Building_Size_And_Construction.description: Building size (gross/net square footage), layout, number of stories, and construction type/materials.

Highest_And_Best_Use.description: The highest and best use conclusions for the property. Find the section discussing 'Highest and Best Use' and extract both the 'As Vacant' conclusion and the 'As Improved' conclusion. Return them together as: 'As Vacant: [conclusion]. As Improved: [conclusion].' If only one conclusion is present, return what is available.

Sales_Comparison_Value.description: Generate the value indication from the Sales Comparison Approach. Compare recent sales of similar properties mentioned in the report, summarize the comparable sales used, adjustments made, and the final indicated value from this approach.

Direct_Capitalization_Value.description: Generate the value indication from the Direct Capitalization method (NOI divided by Cap Rate). Extract the Net Operating Income, the capitalization rate used, and calculate or summarize the resulting value indication from this approach.

DCF_Analysis_Value.description: Generate the value indication from the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis. Summarize the projection period, discount rate, terminal cap rate, and the resulting present value conclusion from this approach.

Cost_Approach_Value.description: Generate the value indication from the Cost Approach. Summarize the land value, replacement/reproduction cost new, applicable depreciation (physical, functional, external), and the resulting value indication (Land value + Replacement cost - Depreciation).

USPAP_Compliance.description: Determine USPAP compliance status by checking for a 'CERTIFICATION' section in the report. Look for a dedicated certification page containing statements such as 'this report has been prepared, in conformity with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice' or 'comply with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice', along with appraiser signatures. If a certification section exists with USPAP compliance statements and appraiser signature(s), return 'Certified & Signed'. If no certification section or USPAP compliance statement is found in the report, return 'No certification available'.
